channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: California Voters Express Uncertainty and Concern Ahead of 2026 Governor Race
item.description: As California's 2026 gubernatorial race nears the primary, voters across the San Gabriel Valley are expressing widespread uncertainty and disillusionment. Democrat Xavier Becerra has unexpectedly rise...

item.title: Two First-Year SUZA Students Tragically Drown at Kizingo Beach
item.description: On Saturday, May 16, 2026, two first-year students from the State University of Zanzibar (SUZA) tragically lost their lives while swimming at Kizingo Beach in Unguja. The students, Thobiasi Chikakalan...

item.title: PM commits to completing all delayed projects in Tanzania
item.description: Tanzania's Prime Minister has reassured citizens and stakeholders that all stalled development projects across the country will be completed on time. During recent statements, the PM highlighted the g...

item.title: Radio as a tool to improve literacy in Nigeria
item.description: Every Saturday for ten years, Nigerians gather around their radios for *Book on Review with Benji*, a show wey dey push the message say 'A nation wey no dey read no fit develop.' Host Benjamin Okoh do...

item.title: Alison Victoria says a single misused word affected her return to HGTV's Battle on the Beach
item.description: Former 'Battle on the Beach' co-host Alison Victoria revealed that a single word she used may have cost her a return to HGTV's seaside design competition. In an interview with the Daily Mail, Victoria...

item.title: Controversy over reopening of Vancouver supervised consumption site
item.description: The B.C. NDP government is planning to reopen the Thomas Donaghy Overdose Prevention Site (TDOPS) in downtown Vancouver, despite opposition from the city’s mayor and council. Previously, the site face...

item.title: Peter Marrone to Chair Challenger Gold and Lead $80 Million Capital Raising

item.title: Ukraine Targets Moscow with Hundreds of Drones, Causing Deaths and Damage
item.description: On May 17, 2026, Moscow experienced one of the largest drone attacks in over a year, as Ukrainian forces reportedly launched more than 500 drones targeting facilities across the Russian capital and su...

item.title: Remembering Jennifer Runyon: Actress and Beloved 1980s Star Passes Away at 65

item.title: IFP Mayoral Candidate Unveils Plan to Revitalise Johannesburg
item.description: Mlungisi Mabaso, the Inkatha Freedom Party's (IFP) candidate for mayor of Johannesburg, has outlined a comprehensive strategy to revitalise South Africa’s largest city. Speaking at Jabulani Technical ...

item.title: Understanding How Stress Affects Your Body and Health
item.description: Stress triggers a complex set of physiological responses designed for survival, including increased heart rate, blood pressure, and the release of adrenaline and cortisol. While these reactions were e...

item.title: Debate on Political Parties Act and Its Impact on Party Autonomy and Democracy
item.description: Stakeholders in Tanzania are raising concerns over the Political Parties Act, 2024, claiming eya restricts political freedom by giving too much power to the Registrar of Political Parties (RPPs). Crit...

item.description: Canberra Raiders fans will be cheering as two of their players, Hudson Young and Ethan Strange, earn selection in the NSW Blues squad for the upcoming State of Origin opener at Homebush on Wednesday, ...

item.title: Traffic resumes on Highway 427 off-ramp to Rutherford Road after overnight accident in Vaughan
item.description: The off-ramp from Highway 427 to Rutherford Road in Vaughan has reopened following an overnight crash that temporarily disrupted traffic. Authorities responded promptly to the incident, ensuring that ...

item.title: International divers assist in recovery of Italian victims from Maldives cave
item.description: A multinational team of expert divers has arrived in the Maldives to recover the bodies of four Italian tourists who died while scuba diving near Vaavu Atoll. The tragedy follows the death of Italian ...

item.title: Ottawa weather forecast calls for hot temperatures and periods of rain
item.description: Ottawa residents can expect hot weather accompanied by some rain over the coming days, according to the latest forecast. As the region transitions into the warmer season, temperatures are set to rise ...

item.title: Letters Discuss Provincial Rights and Section 92A of the Constitution
item.description: This article is a collection of letters to the editor discussing the historical and ongoing importance of Section 92A of the Constitution Act, 1982, which affirms provincial jurisdiction over non-rene...
